task.name: Add Tag model and relations to Prisma schema
task.action: In `prisma/schema.prisma`: 1. Add the `Tag` model with fields: id, userId, name (VarChar(50)), color (VarChar(25)), createdAt, updatedAt. Add `@@unique([userId, name])` and `@@map("tags")`. 2. Add `tags Tag[]` relation field to `ExpenseTransaction`, `IncomeTransaction`, and `TransferTransaction` models. 3. Add `tags Tag[]` relation field to the `User` model.
task.verify: Run `npx prisma validate` — must pass with no errors.

task.name: Generate and apply Prisma migration
task.action: Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name add-tags` to generate and apply the migration. Review the generated SQL to confirm: - `tags` table is created with correct columns and constraints. - Three implicit join tables (`_ExpenseTransactionToTag`, `_IncomeTransactionToTag`, `_TransferTransactionToTag`) are created. - No existing tables are altered destructively.
task.verify: Run `npx prisma migrate status` — must show all migrations applied. Run `npx prisma generate` — must succeed.

task.name: Create GET/POST /api/tags route
task.action: Create `src/app/api/tags/route.ts`: - GET: Authenticate session, return all tags for the user ordered by name, `200`. - POST: Authenticate session, validate body with Zod (`name: string`), count existing user tags to generate color via `hsl((count * 360) / Math.max(count + 1, 1), 65%, 60%)`, create tag with `db.tag.create(...)`, return `201`. - Handle duplicate name error (Prisma unique constraint) with a `409` response.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` — must pass with no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Create DELETE/PATCH /api/tags/[id] route
task.action: Create `src/app/api/tags/[id]/route.ts`: - DELETE: Authenticate, verify tag belongs to user, delete (Prisma cascades disconnect from join tables), return `200`. - PATCH: Authenticate, validate `{ name: string }`, update tag name, return `200`. Handle duplicate name with `409`.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` — must pass with no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Create useTagsQuery hook
task.action: Create `src/hooks/useTagsQuery.ts`: - Use TanStack Query with key `['tags']` to fetch `GET /api/tags`. - Export `tags`, `isLoading`, `createTag(name)` mutation, and `deleteTag(id)` mutation. - Both mutations invalidate the `['tags']` query on success. - Follow the same patterns used in `useExpenseTypesQuery.ts`.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` — must pass with no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Add tagIds to expense transaction POST and PATCH
task.action: 1. In `src/app/api/expense-transactions/route.ts`: - Add `tagIds: z.array(z.string()).max(10).optional()` to `ServerPostExpenseSchema`. - In the POST handler, when creating the expense, add `tags: { connect: tagIds.map(id => ({ id })) }` if tagIds is provided. - Add `include: { tags: true }` to all `findMany` and `create` calls. - Add tag name to the search OR clause so searching works with tags. 2. In `src/app/api/expense-transactions/[id]/route.ts`: - Add `tagIds: z.array(z.string()).max(10).optional()` to `ServerPatchExpenseSchema`. - In the PATCH handler, add `tags: { set: tagIds.map(id => ({ id })) }` to updateData if tagIds is provided. - Add `include: { tags: true }` to GET and PATCH responses.
task.verify: Run `npm run test` — all existing expense transaction tests must still pass. Run `npm run build` — must pass.

task.name: Add tagIds to income transaction POST and PATCH
task.action: Apply the same pattern as expense transactions to: 1. `src/app/api/income-transactions/route.ts` — POST schema + handler + includes. 2. `src/app/api/income-transactions/[id]/route.ts` — PATCH schema + handler + includes + GET includes.
task.verify: Run `npm run test` — all existing income transaction tests must still pass. Run `npm run build` — must pass.

task.name: Add tagIds to transfer transaction POST and PATCH
task.action: Apply the same pattern as expense transactions to: 1. `src/app/api/transfer-transactions/route.ts` — POST schema + handler + includes. 2. `src/app/api/transfer-transactions/[id]/route.ts` — PATCH schema + handler + includes + GET includes.
task.verify: Run `npm run test` — all existing transfer transaction tests must still pass. Run `npm run build` — must pass.

task.name: Build the TagInput component
task.action: Create `src/components/shared/TagInput.tsx`: - Props: `selectedTagIds: string[]`, `onChange: (tagIds: string[]) => void`, `disabled?: boolean`. - Uses `useTagsQuery()` internally to fetch all user tags. - Renders selected tags as `Badge` components (variant="secondary") with: - A small colored circle (`div` with inline `backgroundColor` from tag.color). - Tag name text. - An ✕ (`X` icon from lucide) button to remove. - Shows an `Input` that opens a `Popover` with a `Command` list: - Filters user tags by typed text. - Unselected tags shown as selectable items. - A "Create [typed-text]" action at the bottom when no exact name match exists. - Clicking "Create" calls `createTag(name)` from `useTagsQuery` and adds the new tag's ID. - When 10 tags are selected, the input is hidden and a small "(max 10)" label is shown.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` — must pass with no TypeScript errors.

task.name: Add TagInput to all Expense forms
task.action: 1. In `CreateExpenseTransactionDrawer.tsx` and `CreateExpenseTransactionSheet.tsx`: - Add `tagIds: []` to form defaultValues. - Add a `FormField` for `tagIds` rendering `TagInput` after the description field. - Pass `tagIds` in the submit payload. 2. In `EditExpenseDrawer.tsx` (and Sheet if exists): - Initialize `tagIds` from the existing transaction's tags. - Add a `FormField` for `tagIds` rendering `TagInput`. - Pass `tagIds` in the update payload.

task.name: Add TagInput to all Income forms
task.action: Apply the same TagInput integration pattern to: - `CreateIncomeTransactionDrawer.tsx` and `CreateIncomeTransactionSheet.tsx` - `EditIncomeDrawer.tsx` (and Sheet if exists)

task.name: Add TagInput to all Transfer forms
task.action: Apply the same TagInput integration pattern to: - `CreateTransferDrawer.tsx` and `CreateTransferSheet.tsx` - `EditTransferDrawer.tsx` (and Sheet if exists)

task.name: Update transaction query hooks to include tags
task.action: Update the TypeScript interfaces/types in: - `src/hooks/useExpenseTransactionsQuery.ts` — add `tags: { id: string; name: string; color: string }[]` to the transaction type. - `src/hooks/useIncomeTransactionsQuery.ts` — same. - `src/hooks/useTransferTransactionsQuery.ts` — same. No query key or fetch logic changes needed since the API response now includes tags automatically.

task.name: Display tags on CompactTransactionCard
task.action: In `src/components/transactions/CompactTransactionCard.tsx`: 1. Add `tags?: { id: string; name: string; color: string }[]` to `CompactTransactionCardProps`. 2. Below the category/account line, conditionally render a flex-wrap row of small `Badge` components for each tag: - `Badge` variant="outline", extra small text - Inline colored dot from `tag.color` - Tag name 3. Keep the row subtle — `text-[10px]`, `gap-1`, `mt-1`, max 3 visible with a "+N more" indicator if needed.
task.verify: Run `npm run test` — existing `CompactTransactionCard.test.tsx` must pass (tags prop is optional). Run `npm run build` — must pass.

task.name: Run full test suite and build
task.action: 1. Run `npm run test` — all unit tests must pass. 2. Run `npm run build` — production build must succeed with no errors. 3. Run `npm run lint` — no new lint warnings.
task.verify: All three commands exit with code 0.

task.name: Manual smoke test
task.action: With the dev server running (`npm run dev`): 1. Create a new expense transaction and add 2 tags ("Bangkok", "Food"). Confirm tags appear as colored pills in the form and are saved. 2. Edit an old expense transaction (one created before this feature). Add a tag ("Test"). Confirm it saves and displays. 3. Create a new income transaction with 1 tag. Confirm tags display on the transaction card. 4. Create a new transfer with 1 tag. Confirm tags display. 5. Open the expense list, confirm tags are visible on CompactTransactionCard. 6. Try to add an 11th tag — confirm the input is hidden and the limit message appears. 7. Remove a tag from an existing transaction — confirm it updates correctly.
task.verify: All 7 manual checks pass as described.
